subject: Search Dashboard Courses & Programs - Output Fields
description:
Hello, 

An end user brought to my attention that the search, for both courses and programs, are not populating correctly. I have adjusted the output fields and have saved these corrected settings as the new default search to no avail. Can I please get some assistance? Thank you! 

Best,
Armando

comments.body:
Hello, 

An end user brought to my attention that the search, for both courses and programs, are not populating correctly. I have adjusted the output fields and have saved these corrected settings as the new default search to no avail. Can I please get some assistance? Thank you! 

Best,
Armando

Armando,

This can happen when course forms get versioned and the course search doesn't have the new updated ID's for every user individual saved default search. Current way to fix this is to go to **Output Fields**, select the page where fields exist, **Basic Course Information**, check **Course Discipline**, **Course Number**, **Course Title**, and click **Search**. You should then see the columns, and then you can click & drag/drop the columns to be in the order that you want, then click on the little square button to the right of "**My Searches**", then click on "**Save this search as the default search for Courses**", then click "**Save**", then this should fix the search. Each user that is having the issue will need to do this if the "**Reset**" button is not working for them. See image examples below.

* * *

Armando,

In that previous ticket, I explained how programs filters off user/position filtered by department and courses filters off user/position filtered by subject.

Looking at this user "**Stephanie Briano**", I see this user does have this position "**Curriculum Area Representative**", and it's filtered by department "**Noncredit**", but this user also has the same position filtered off subject "**APAR**", which is mapped to department "**Child Development**", and it looks like it pulls the user in due to that user/position filtered mapping to subject for programs.

When the lower filtered tier of subject exists in a user/position mapping, it includes it in the workflow process when user/position is added to the proposal when it moves to step/level 5 for programs.

If you remove that user/position subject filter, then when proposal moves to step/level 5, it will no longer add that user to that position for step/level 5 for this program "**Elementary Teacher Education: Integrated Programs**" (1015).

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/Z9SP2OrRPHk9wg0PgMUK6PS2V/?name=image.png)

Armando,

We have received this support ticket, and I will need to discuss this ticket with our support team. I will get back with you once I know more.

Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225
E: mike.gregston@acadea.com | W: www.acadea.com
A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

Talked with **Clinton Worle** and found out that when updating the user/position filtered by department, it only updates proposal workflows based off that user/position filtered by department, it doesn't include user/position filter by subject, like it does when you administratively move a proposal from one level and then back, it checks both tiers for user/position, doesn't matter it is a program.

User/Position tier filtering should be all by department or all by subject. Then we can guarantee the user/position that would be added by the hierarchy mapping.

If user/position is filtered by mix tiers, then there can be unexpected behavior at times depending on what all they have the user/position filtered by.

Armando,

In that previous ticket, I explained how programs filters off user/position filtered by department and courses filters off user/position filtered by subject. This is true as long as when you filter a user/position only by one tier, but when you filter a user/position by multiple tiers, it then can have mixed behavior like you're seeing now.

Looking at this user "**Stephanie Briano**", I see this user does have this position "**Curriculum Area Representative**", and it's filtered by department "**Noncredit**", but this user also has the same position filtered off subject "**APAR**", which is mapped to department "**Child Development**", and that is why it pulls the user in, due to that user/position filtered mapping to subject for programs.

When the lower filter tier of subject exists in a user/position mapping for programs, it includes the departments mapped to those subjects for that user/position for programs.

If you remove that user/position subject filter, then when proposal moves to step/level 5, it will no longer add that user to that position for step/level 5 for this program "**Elementary Teacher Education: Integrated Programs**" (1015).

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/Z9SP2OrRPHk9wg0PgMUK6PS2V/?name=image.png)

Best practice is to use just one tier for user/position for filtering. If you just filter the user position by department it works well for programs. If you just filter the user position by subject it works well for courses. If you're going to mix the position filtered tiers, then it still works for courses where subject is the lowest tier, but not so well for programs if those multiple subjects have multiple departments.

If you filter user position by department and subject for programs, it will still use that department, but it will also pull any department the subjects are mapped to for that user/position.

These are some of the different options you can do to try to fix this user/position for programs:

1. Remove all user/position filtered by subject and only filter by department for programs to work based off department. Note, this would affect user/position in course workflows, which you may not want.
2. Remove all user/position filtered by department and only filter by subject for programs. Note, this would affect user/position in program workflows, which could be what you want, but depends on which subject is mapped to which department.
3. Duplicate the position, have one for course workflows, and the other for program workflows, would have to update all users that have this position to either have one or the other, then each position per user would have to be updated to have the correct tier mappings for that position per what workflow type they go to, and would have to update workflows to use the correct position where needed. One position you would only filter by department and the other you would only filter by subject to keep them separate between program and course workflows.

Note, any changes to this user/position can affect courses and program workflows differently depending on which way you go.

Support Supervisor

description:

comments.body:
Hello, 

At the request of the Vice President of Instruction, I'm inquiring about a recently deployed functionality to automatically updated course information when it is activated. Is there a way to pause this functionality? There are concerns on how to capture data changes and requires further conversation/vetting. Thank you in advance!

Additional email received -

Good afternoon. I hope you and yours are well! I'm reaching out to request some additional assistance. I submitted a ticket to inquire if there was a way to pause the automated updates to program proposals when courses are activated? There is a desire to pause this feature as we continue to have further discussions revolving about being able to leverage the functionality in a way that permits us to capture the changes and slate them for approval with the appropriate approving bodies.
Thank you in advance for any and all insight and assistance you can provide.

My response -

There is a solution; however, there is one problem.
If you need us to turn it back on, there is no way to automatically update any of the programs that weren’t updated while it was turned off. Writing a script to handle this has proven problematic. You would need to identify which programs require updates and apply modifications manually.

**Natalie Rasmussen** I see that you mentioned not being able to go back to systemically make the updates that auto update would have handled.  Is staff aware of where and what will no longer be updated by removing the auto update functionality?  To prevent downstream issues, I'd like their buy-in on the list of updates that will not happen.  In the meantime - we will triage.

I spoke with Debra concerning this and they are very much aware of the issues and nothing will update in the requisistes and programs.

They had a third party company do a audit of this function and according to them, this is completely ourt of complaince with the state of California and you cannot auto update.
